items.title: Hegseth, Caine Testify in Support of Supplemental Budget Request

items.title: Army Contracting Support Enables Marine Operations Along Southern Border
items.content_text: Army contracting support is essential for the Marine Corps' mission at the U.S. southern border. By walking the ground alongside the warfighters, the contracting team is better positioned to deliver the right capabilities, at the right time, in the right place.

items.title: U.S., Canadian, Thai Navies Sharpen Maritime Capabilities During Exercise CARAT Thailand
items.content_text: The United States, Thailand and Canada successfully concluded the Cooperation Afloat Readiness and Training Thailand maritime exercise in Sattahip, enhancing combined naval capabilities and reinforcing a shared commitment to a free and secure Pacific region.

items.title: Medal of Honor Recipient Welcomes Newest Student Naval Aviators
items.content_text: Retired Navy Capt. E. Royce Williams, the oldest living Medal of Honor recipient, returned to the base where he earned his gold wings, joining Naval Aviation Schools Command in congratulating 29 student naval aviators who completed their introductory flight evaluation training.

items.title: First Guardians in part-time work roles join the Space Force
items.content_text: The Space Force integrated 18 space-experienced Air Force Reservists into part-time Guardian roles, marking the first time Guardians will serve on non-sustained duty as part of a new single-component service structure.

items.title: West Coast Gray Whale Population Increases
items.content_text: West Coast Gray Whale Population Increases Gray whales were nearly hunted to extinction by commercial whaling. Protections under the MMPA, ESA, and the end of commercial whaling have allowed the species to recover. (Image credit: NOAA Fisheries (Permit #19091).) Download Image July 17, 2026 0 Off

items.title: Wyoming Air Guardsmen Activate for Wildland Firefighting in Western U.S.
items.content_text: The Wyoming Air National Guard's 153rd Airlift Wing was activated to support wildland firefighting operations in the western United States, with significant fire activity in the Southwest, Great Basin, Rocky Mountain and Northwest geographic areas.

items.title: Statement by Chief Pentagon Spokesman, Sean Parnell, on Enhanced Screening Protocol to Optimize Warfighter Performance and Enhance Force Readiness
items.content_text: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth directed an enhanced testosterone deficiency screening protocol for service members to optimize performance and maximize mission readiness.

items.title: U.S. Forces Continue Providing Disaster Relief Assistance in Venezuela
items.content_text: U.S. armed forces are continuing to provide disaster relief assistance to the people of Venezuela in the aftermath of two devastating June 24 earthquakes.

items.title: Declassification of the Department of War Internal After-Action Review of the Coronavirus Disease 2019 Vaccine Mandate Report
items.content_text: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth directed the National Academy of Public Administration to conduct an after-action review capturing lessons learned to improve future policy.

items.title: Air Force conducts live-fire test for Collaborative Combat Aircraft program
items.content_text: The Department of the Air Force successfully conducted a live-fire test of a YFQ-44A Collaborative Combat Aircraft, firing an AIM-120 weapon at a digital target. The test demonstrates program maturity and operational validation while emphasizing the retention of human oversight for weapon release.

items.title: CSAF, CMSAF discuss readiness, quality of life during Aviano AB visit
items.content_text: CSAF Wilsbach and CMSAF Wolfe visited Aviano Air Base to discuss readiness and quality-of-life initiatives with Airmen of the 31st Fighter Wing.

items.title: Department of War Announces Landmark Agreements With New Entrants to Accelerate Low-Cost, Air-Launched Cruise Missiles
items.content_text: The Department of War announced framework agreements with three industry partners to complete testing and qualification of the Family of Affordable Mass Missiles Air Force program.

items.title: Special Event Security, Part Two: How the FBI Keeps the Public Safe at Gatherings
items.content_text: The Bureau plays a critical role in keeping everyone safe at public gatherings defined as special events by the U.S. Department of Homeland Security. Qualifying events can be almost any kind of public gathering: parades, local fairs, music festivals, foot races, concerts, awards shows, and sporting events, just to name a few.

items.title: Hegseth Hosts Iraqi Prime Minister at Pentagon
items.content_text: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth welcomed Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi to the Pentagon, where the two leaders met to discuss the Global Coalition to Defeat ISIS, the end of the Operation Inherent Resolve mission and the future of the U.S.-Iraq relationship.

items.title: DHA Partners With Marines to Refine Portable TBI Assessment Technology for Austere Battlefield
items.content_text: Team members assigned to the Defense Health Agency's Warfighter Readiness, Performance and Brain Health initiative hosted an end user touchpoint at Camp Lejeune, N.C., to assess new capabilities for evaluating traumatic brain injuries on the battlefield.

items.title: The Department of War's Office of Strategic Capital Introduces National Security Fund Finance Program
items.content_text: The Department of War's Office of Strategic Capital announced the introduction of the National Security Fund Finance program, which aims to provide capital support to credit funds addressing shortages, gaps and vulnerabilities in critical minerals vital to U.S. national security.

items.title: Readout of Secretary of War Pete Hegseth's Meeting With Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi
items.content_text: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth met with Iraq's Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi at the Pentagon to discuss the global coalition to defeat ISIS, the end of the Operation Inherent Resolve mission from Iraq and the future of the U.S.-Iraq relationship.

items.title: Guardian astronaut brings operational medicine experience to ISS mission
items.content_text: U.S. Space Force Col. Anil Menon launched aboard NASA’s Expedition 75 mission to the International Space Station, bringing with him a career shaped by operational medicine, military service and human spaceflight support.

items.title: Skyfall Simulation: NATO Partners Rehearse Downed Aircraft Response
items.content_text: Kosovo Force Regional Command East's Task Force Aviation and multinational partners conducted a downed aircraft exercise near Camp Bondsteel, Kosovo, rehearsing search and rescue, medical evacuation and aircraft recovery procedures following a simulated UH-60 Black Hawk helicopter hard landing.

items.title: Department of War Issues Final FY26 APFIT Awards, Surpassing $2 Billion Milestone and Debuting Software Capabilities
items.content_text: The War Department announced a new round of awards under the Accelerate the Procurement and Fielding of Innovative Technologies program, marking a major milestone as the program surpasses $2 billion in total awards since its inception.

items.title: Massachusetts Man Convicted of Violating U.S. Sanctions Against Iran
items.content_text: A Massachusetts man was convicted in federal court in Boston following a 14-day jury trial for charges related to a scheme to illegally export sophisticated electronic components from the United States to Iran.

items.title: War Department Changes Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification Requirements
items.content_text: The War Department announced the suspension of the Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification phase two requirements, initially scheduled to take effect in November, and launched a comprehensive review of the entire CMMC program.

items.title: Space Force launches Initial Enlisted Bonus Pilot Program to maximize readiness, return on investment
items.content_text: The United States Space Force has announced implementation of a temporary Initial Enlistment Bonus Pilot Program, offering a $25,000 bonus to incoming enlisted Guardians who commit to an eight-year active-duty contract.

items.title: Forging the Arsenal of Freedom: Department of War Suspends CMMC Phase II Requirements
items.content_text: The Department of War announced the immediate suspension of the Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification Phase II requirements, which were originally scheduled to come into effect on Nov. 10, 2026.

items.title: 14 Guardians graduate inaugural Regional Space Advisor Academics Course
items.content_text: The U.S. Space Force graduated 14 Guardians from its first Regional Space Advisor Academics Course at the Pentagon. The program aims to develop international affairs and security cooperation skills within the Space Force to strengthen global partnerships and support the National Defense Strategy.
